name: Beretta MAB 38
summary: Considered one of the finest submachine guns of the war. Adopted by Italian, German, and Romanian forces. Its double-trigger design (one for semi-auto, one for full-auto) was innovative.

name: Breda M30
summary: Italy's standard squad automatic weapon. Criticised for its unusual 20-round feed strip (which had to be oiled before loading) and tendency to jam. Replaced gradually by the Breda M37.

name: Breda M37
summary: Italy's standard medium machine gun from 1937. More reliable than the M30. Used a tray feed system; fired cartridges were reinserted into the feed tray (a distinctive feature).

name: Carcano M91/38
summary: The standard Italian infantry rifle. The calibre was changed from 6.5mm to 7.35mm in 1938 for better stopping power, but wartime shortages meant many soldiers continued using older 6.5mm weapons.
